description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring a prominent array of dark, rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust, angular support structure. The panels are arranged in a slightly tilted orientation, likely to maximize sunlight exposure, and are bordered by a metallic frame. The base structure appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and reinforced materials, with several block-like supports and a few colored accent elements, including blue and red highlights. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, while the supporting frame and base have a matte, industrial finish. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments, with ample surface area for energy collection. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent, and the structure is not designed for entry or traversal by agents.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with a tilted, dark photovoltaic surface and a sturdy metallic support structure, accented with blue and red elements. The installation is industrial in appearance and optimized for solar energy collection.
description_view_0: From this view, the tilted solar panel surface and the angular support structure are most prominent, with the blue and red accent elements visible along the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the length of the solar array and the block-like supports beneath the panels, as well as the layered construction of the base.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the panel array and the full width of the support structure, with the metallic frame and base details clearly visible.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the panel tilt and the arrangement of the support blocks, with blue accents at the far end of the structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic frame and block supports
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 large panels
